exit_criteria: the alpha.4 request, persisted-challenge retry, hello, atomic one-time consumption transcript and signing objects remain byte stable
challenge requests stay rate-limited, limited to active commissioned Gateway credential bindings, non-authenticating, and disabled by default
delivery and heartbeat uplinks use their frozen wire-to-signing projections, explicit freshness input, and conflict-safe replay state

a committed delivery stores one session-independent immutable digest and one current-session binding; only an exact same-session replay or strictly higher active session epoch may recover a receipt without repeating the business effect
the business effect and initial replay record commit atomically, a pending authentication record never bypasses first-acceptance expiry, and a rebind is durable before its current-session receipt
the four-field wire replay identity is looked up only inside the validated authorization-namespace partition, and cross-namespace or global unpartitioned record reuse is rejected
each MQTT prefix is an authorization namespace with one unique Gateway identity and exact principal topic scope
production key provisioning, rotation, revocation, and verifier ownership are implemented
generic shared-Broker deliveries prove publisher identity per accepted publish through one declared origin model
topic, payload identity, and MQTT credentials are never sufficient authentication
valid and invalid fixtures pass every conformant binding
the Cloud session-accepted response has a frozen signing projection and Cloud key lifecycle
the response cryptographically binds the accepted challenge and client request so delayed or replayed responses from another handshake are rejected
valid, invalid, delayed, replayed, and cross-handshake fixtures pass every conformant binding
envelope, time, identity, digest, and ACK schemas have one authority
the explicitly activated Integration extension reuses the same session, position, replay, digest, data-loss, and ACK semantics
a cumulative ACK advances only across a contiguous persisted or declared-lost prefix
all language bindings report the same failure classes
TypeScript, Rust, C, and C++ execute the same fixture manifest
canonical bytes and digests are byte identical
one real Broker runs an edge harness and Cloud ingress concurrently
session resume, Runtime Manifest, and telemetry traverse application use cases
disconnect, ACK loss, out-of-order persistence, prefix gaps, late gap fill, process restart, duplicate delivery, digest conflict, and data-loss pass
no successful receipt is produced for a conflict or uncommitted fact
the signed ACK schema and Cloud signing transcript are frozen
successful ACK creation and outbox insertion share the production fact transaction
post-commit crash recovery republishes the identical ACK
every preceding gate passes
legacy remains available for rollback
core CloudLink contains no physical-control operation and every optional physical-effect extension remains independently gated
